description_long: This is a vintage-style corded telephone with a polished, dark metallic finish and ornate detailing. The base is rectangular with rounded edges and supports a rotary dial on the front, featuring white number markers and a circular finger wheel. The handset is cylindrical with metallic end caps and is connected to the base by a coiled cord, resting on a decorative cradle with two upward arms. The earpiece and mouthpiece are both bell-shaped, matching the metallic aesthetic of the rest of the phone. The phone's design is reminiscent of early 20th-century telecommunication devices, with a robust and elegant appearance. The overall construction suggests a combination of metal and plastic, with internal air gaps for the electronics and acoustic chambers.
description: A vintage, ornate corded telephone with a rotary dial, metallic finish, and a coiled cord connecting the handset to the base. The design features decorative elements and a robust, elegant appearance.
description_view_0: Side view showing the coiled cord extending from the left and the handset resting on the cradle, with the rotary dial partially visible.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the rotary dial, number markers, and the full cradle supporting the handset.
description_view_2: Opposite side view with the coiled cord extending to the right and the handset's cylindrical grip visible, rotary dial edge barely seen.
description_view_3: Rear angled view showing the back of the handset, both cradle arms, and the coiled cord trailing forward.
